title: GPTZero investigation says KPMG agentic AI report contained widespread flawed citations
friendlyTitle: A KPMG AI report shows why citations still need checking
summary: GPTZero published an investigation alleging that KPMG’s agentic AI report contained extensive citation problems, including fake or distorted references and misattributed claims later summarized by City AM and other outlets.
friendlySummary: GPTZero says a KPMG report about agentic AI had many flawed citations and questionable claims. KPMG reportedly removed the report and said it is reviewing what happened.
whyItMatters: AI can speed up research and reporting, but professional output still needs source verification. The practical lesson for every team is simple: citation checks and human review are not optional when AI-generated work leaves the draft stage.
friendlyWhyItMatters: AI can help write reports, but it can also produce confident-looking mistakes. Before sharing important work, someone still needs to verify the sources.
